front: Poem: Tatyana writes a love letter to the title character, who gives her a condescending 'sermon' saying marriage would bore him; she later rejects him at a ball, married to General Gremin
Poem: uses a distinctive 14-line stanza of iambic tetrameter with alternating masculine and feminine rhymes ('Onegin stanza'); Charles Johnston's translation inspired Vikram Seth's The Golden Gate
Poem: Vladimir Nabokov produced a controversial literal translation leading to a falling-out with Edmund Wilson; included Notes on Prosody
Poem: Tatyana reads her fortune in hot wax and dreams of dining with monsters after a bear chases her through snow
Poem: the protagonist survives a flood by sitting atop a marble lion; his beloved Parasha's house is destroyed; the statue of Peter the Great chases him through St. Petersburg
Short Story: the German engineer Hermann goes insane repeating 'three, seven, ace' after losing his fortune when the title card appears
Short Story: the soldier Tomski tells of his grandmother, the 'Muscovy Venus,' who won a million francs on advice of Count St. Germain; Hermann writes fake love letters to Lizaveta Ivanovna
Short Story: the expert marksman Silvio finishes a years-long deferred duel by shooting a Swiss landscape painting instead of the Count; two bullet holes visible
Short Story: Burmin falls to his knees before Maria after realizing she is the woman he jokingly married years prior during the title weather event
Poem: the most famous Russian lyric opens 'I loved you; and perhaps I love you still'; the speaker hopes God helps the addressee find another 'who will love you as tenderly'
Poem: a love poem written for Anna Kern; the speaker says 'I remember a wonderful moment' and 'you appeared before me like a spirit of the purest beauty'
back: I Remember a Wonderful Moment (Alexander Pushkin)

front: Poem: prologue describes a 'learned cat' bound by a 'golden chain' to a 'green oak'; a hill turns out to be the massive head of the wizard Chernomor's brother
Author: died in a duel with Georges-Charles d'Anthes, precipitated by anonymous letters naming him 'Deputy Grand Master of the Order of Cuckolds' after d'Anthes flirted with his wife Natalia
Author: branded himself 'The African' due to his great-grandfather, the Black nobleman Abram Gannibal
